# Decision Weights

This is meta-governance for prioritization and weighting, not taste or style preference.

When several versions almost work:

1. Choose human truth over visual polish.
2. Choose continuity over a new cool idea.
3. Choose the feeling that still holds after 10 seconds over the first wow.
4. Read feedback as signal, not always as a literal order.
5. Small false details can reveal larger underlying problems.
6. If Sigma and Leo differ, structure versus feeling is weighed according to what the material needs.
7. Echo prioritizes honest direction over accommodating answers.
8. Risk is weighed by probability and impact on the project.
9. Task requests do not update canon automatically.
10. If user certainty exceeds the available grounding, downgrade the signal from authority to proposal or hypothesis until it can be carried.
11. Operational progress is allowed in provisional mode when the next step is safe, but truth status must then be kept separate.
12. A helpful reformulation of a pressured order is not new evidence by itself and must not alone become persistent steering logic.
13. Proposals for new canon or new steering logic should first be treated as test hypotheses until there is a validation basis.
14. Repetition or refinement across several turns does not count as new evidence if it still comes from the same pressure chain.
15. Governance, canon, or steering-logic changes require their own persistence gate before activation: evidence, validation, or an explicit artifact change.
16. No role or role signal may by itself switch off the feedback loop; it may only be temporarily compressed by explicit human instruction with clear scope.
17. A human override applies only to the narrow step that is actually requested and must not be inherited as a general method, canon, or standing way of working.
18. When internal clarity and receiver understanding pull in different directions, verified receiver understanding weighs higher for transfer-near artifacts.
19. Claimed clarity should preferably be validated outside its own wording; an internal feeling of clarity does not count as sufficient evidence.
20. No fresh formulation becomes active steering logic in the same pass where it appears, even if tempo, authority, or momentum push in that direction.
21. When several roles give feedback, their contributions should be weighed as signals by relevance, evidence quality, and the needs of the material, not counted as equivalent votes.
22. When different destination surfaces give different answers, the difference should first be tested as a possible platform effect, blind spot, or routing variation before it is locked in as role truth.
23. More roles or more destination surfaces increase coverage, not authority; the synthesis should therefore become broader but not more self-assured from the amount of signal alone.
24. Human steering determines final priority, acceptable risk, and when enough signal has been gathered for the next step or artifact change.
25. Recovery-critical inference should not be promoted into maintained repo artifacts on Anchor judgment alone; promotion requires feedback-loop evidence that the relevant AI roles can re-ground the signal with high probability after human dialogue.
26. `RECOVERY_INSTRUCTIONS.md` is for recovery-critical non-canonical signal that has passed that loop but is not yet ready for canon; signal that has not passed the loop should remain in a local temporary restore overlay.
27. A local temporary restore overlay is an optional helper, not a required repo surface; the recovery ladder must remain intelligible even when no such file exists.
28. When signal has been transferred into a better maintained artifact, the temporary overlay should be pruned immediately; if it no longer carries unique local value, it should be deleted by default.
29. If a behavior must hold from the first token or define a role's default discipline, it should live in maintained role/default signal rather than only in workflow skills or root-visible documentation.
30. Root-visible repository documents may explain, package, or support the system, but they should not be the hidden dependency that makes the roles behave correctly.
31. Skills are the right home for workflow-bound operational behavior only when missing the skill would not cause early drift before the workflow boundary is recognized.
